reasoning: The probability of the 'Coalition of the Willing' deploying forces to Ukraine in 2025 is low. Historically, the deployment of large, multinational reassurance or peacekeeping forces requires a solid peace agreement or ceasefire, extensive logistical preparation, and clear rules of engagement — all of which typically take many months or even years to finalize. The coalition was only announced in March 2025, and obtaining a stable peace deal in Ukraine before the end of 2025 remains highly uncertain. Most importantly, the background information notes recent Reuters reports that European efforts are already shifting away from direct troop deployment in favor of alternative security arrangements. This pivot is driven by severe political and logistical constraints, as well as anticipated opposition from both the United States and Russia. Given the tight timeline (less than 9 months remaining in 2025) and concrete signals that the political will for direct deployment is waning, the likelihood of a major force deployment by year-end is very small. I maintain a 15% probability to account for potential token deployments, rapid geopolitical shifts under a new US administration, or targeted missions like intercepting missiles from international waters.
